VERANDA LEARNING: VERANDA RACE PARTNERS WITH DR. RAJKUMAR ACADEMY TO EXPAND GOVT EXAM COACHING IN KARNATAKA 📚🤝

• 🤝 Veranda RACE has signed an MoU with Dr. Rajkumar Academy for Civil Services (DRACS), integrating the two institutions to strengthen government exam coaching across Karnataka.  

• 🎯 The partnership covers academic operations, brand stewardship, government & institutional partnerships, while continuing DRACS' social-impact initiatives under the Veranda RACE umbrella. 

• 🏛️ DRACS has built a strong presence over the last nine years in coaching for UPSC, KAS, KPSC and other State Public Service Commission examinations from its Bengaluru centre. 

• 🌐 The collaboration will also continue DRACS' partnerships with Government of Karnataka departments, UNDP, UNICEF and NGOs, while leveraging Veranda RACE's technology, academic expertise and operational scale to expand access across the state. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The partnership strengthens Veranda Learning's presence in the government exam coaching segment, expands its reach in Karnataka and enhances its institutional network.)

Dr. Lal PathLabs Update

Board Approvals
- Interim dividend: ₹5/share.
- Record date: July 30, 2026.

Key Highlights
- Acquires 80% stake in Ghana's Sunshine Healthcare.
- Invests up to ₹3.5 Cr in Neuome Technologies.
- Strengthens diagnostics & AI capabilities.
- Approved 1.19 lakh employee stock options.

Key Takeaway
- Positive: The Ghana acquisition, strategic technology investment, and interim dividend support Dr. Lal PathLabs' long-term growth and shareholder value.
MARINE ELECTRICALS; WINS RS 376 CR ORDER

Godawari Power & Ispat Update

Stake Sale
- To sell 16.75 lakh JPL shares.
- Deal value: ~₹49.99 crore.

Key Highlights
- Sale price: ₹298.45/share.
- JPL stake to reduce to 35.36%.
- Completion expected by Aug 30, 2026.
- Not a related-party transaction.

Key Takeaway
- Positive: The stake sale unlocks ~₹50 crore, improves liquidity, and optimizes GPIL's investment portfolio while retaining a significant stake in Jammu Pigments.
APSIS AEROCOM: BAGS ₹81.57 LAKH INTERNATIONAL DEFENCE ORDER

APSIS AEROCOM: BAGS ₹81.57 LAKH INTERNATIONAL DEFENCE ORDER 🌍🛡️

• 🏆 Apsis Aerocom has received a purchase order from an international customer in the defence segment. The customer name has not been disclosed due to confidentiality obligations.  

• 💰 The order value is USD 84,484 (approximately ₹81.57 lakh based on the exchange rate at the time of filing). 

• 📅 The order is scheduled to be executed by February 14, 2027. 

• 🤝 The company confirmed that the order is not a related-party transaction, and the promoter/promoter group has no interest in the awarding entity. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The international defence order strengthens Apsis Aerocom's order book, enhances its global presence and supports future revenue visibility.)

GODAWARI POWER & ISPAT: TO SELL STAKE IN JAMMU PIGMENTS FOR ₹49.99 CRORE 💰🏭

• 🤝 The Board has approved the partial divestment of 16.75 lakh equity shares held in Jammu Pigments Ltd. (JPL) to the promoters of JPL and/or their relatives or affiliates. 

• 💰 The shares will be sold at a fair value of ₹298.45 per share, aggregating to approximately ₹49.99 crore. 

• 📉 Following the transaction, Godawari Power & Ispat's stake in JPL will reduce from 43.96% (85.70 lakh shares) to 35.36% (68.95 lakh shares). 

• 📅 The company expects the transaction to be completed on or before August 30, 2026. The sale is not a related-party transaction for GPIL. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The stake sale is expected to unlock around ₹50 crore of value, strengthen liquidity and optimize GPIL's investment portfolio while retaining a significant stake in Jammu Pigments.)

DR. LAL PATHLABS: APPROVES GHANA ACQUISITION, AI INVESTMENT & ₹5 INTERIM DIVIDEND 🌍🧬

• 💰 Board approved an interim dividend of ₹5 per equity share (50% on face value of ₹10). The record date is July 30, 2026, and the dividend will be paid within 30 days. 

• 🌍 The Board took note that its Dubai wholly owned subsidiary Dr. Lal PathLabs FZCO has approved the acquisition of an 80% stake in Sunshine Healthcare Ltd., Ghana, for a consideration of up to GHS 45.60 million, strengthening its international diagnostics presence.  

• 🧬 The Board also took note that Dr. Lal Ventures Pvt. Ltd. has approved the acquisition of a 30% stake in Neuome Technologies Pvt. Ltd. for up to ₹3.5 crore, to invest in sample preservation, pre-analytical technologies and biobanking solutions.  

• 👨‍💼 The company also approved the grant of 1,19,300 stock options under the Dr. Lal PathLabs Employee Restricted Stock Unit Plan 2025 for eligible employees.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (The international expansion, strategic technology investment and interim dividend reinforce Dr. Lal PathLabs' long-term growth strategy and shareholder returns.)

PAYTM: SAIF PARTNERS SAID TO SELL STAKE IN PAYTM 5-8% DISCOUNT

Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz appears to have dealt far greater damage to its own trade with China than to Beijing's commerce with the rest of the Persian Gulf, according to newly released Chinese customs data.

Between March and June 2026, Iran's non-oil trade with China totaled just $830 million, down from $3.3 billion during the same period last year—a decline of roughly 75%.

Kpler shipping data reviewed by Iran International also point to a sharp decline in Iranian oil exports to China. 

Average daily unloadings at Chinese ports fell from 1.74 million barrels per day in April to around 550,000 barrels per day in the first half of July.

Together, the figures suggest the Strait of Hormuz closure has dealt a severe blow to Iran's economic ties with its largest trading partner.

ICE MAKE REFRIGERATION: SIGNS STRATEGIC JV WITH JAPAN'S GALILEI; TO RAISE ₹190 CRORE VIA PREFERENTIAL ISSUE 🇮🇳🇯🇵❄️

• 🤝 Board approved Share Subscription Agreement (SSA), Shareholders' Agreement (SHA) and Joint Venture Agreement (JVA) with Japan's Galilei Holdings Co. Ltd. 

• 🏭 To incorporate Ice Make Horeca Pvt. Ltd., which will manufacture and market commercial refrigerators, table-top refrigerators, ice makers, modular blast chillers, and provide after-sales services. The JV will be 60% owned by Galilei and 40% by Ice Make after fund infusion. 

• 💰 Board approved a preferential issue of 23.68 lakh equity shares at ₹802.51/share, raising ₹190 crore. The proposed investors will hold 13.05% of the post-issue diluted equity, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals. 

• 🗳️ Board also approved amendments to the Articles of Association, grant of shareholder rights to Galilei, and scheduled an EGM on 19 August 2026 to seek approvals. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The strategic partnership with Galilei brings global technology, strengthens Ice Make's commercial refrigeration portfolio, and the ₹190 crore capital infusion supports future growth and expansion.)

AMBIKA COTTON MILLS: ANNOUNCES ₹135 CRORE MODERNISATION PLAN; EXPANSION FUNDED THROUGH INTERNAL ACCRUALS 🏭🧵

• 🏗️ Ambika Cotton has announced a ₹135 crore modernization plan for Unit IV, upgrading 43,000 spindles to 45,000 spindles with state-of-the-art machinery. The entire investment will be funded through internal accruals. 

• ⚙️ The company also informed that the 6,048-spindle expansion is already operational, while an additional 6,480 spindles are nearing completion. The ₹80 crore expansion capex has also been fully funded through internal accruals. 

• 📅 The modernization project is scheduled to commence in November 2026 and is expected to be completed by March 2027. 

• 📈 The company stated that these initiatives will strengthen its production base, improve productivity, and enhance product quality. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The capacity expansion and modernization, funded entirely through internal accruals, strengthen operational efficiency, improve product quality and support long-term growth.)

SHILP GRAVURES: TEMPORARILY SUSPENDS MANUFACTURING DUE TO FLOODING 🌧️🏭

• ⚠️ Shilp Gravures has temporarily suspended operations at its Gandhinagar manufacturing facility with effect from July 24, 2026, due to flooding caused by incessant heavy rainfall and severe waterlogging. 

• 🛠️ The shutdown has been undertaken to facilitate dewatering, inspection, cleaning and restoration activities, with the company expecting to progressively resume normal operations at the earliest. 

• 🛡️ The company said the affected assets are adequately covered under insurance, and it has initiated the insurance claim process. 

• 📊 The extent of damage to plant, machinery and inventory is still being assessed, and the financial impact cannot be quantified at this stage due to restricted access to parts of the facility. 

🟡 Impact: Neutral (The disruption is temporary and insured, but the financial impact remains uncertain until the damage assessment is completed.)

ZF STEERING GEAR: RAISES ALUMINIUM PROJECT INVESTMENT TO ₹150 CRORE 🏭⚙️

• 🚀 ZF Steering Gear's wholly owned subsidiary DriveSys Systems Pvt. Ltd. has revised the project cost for its Aluminium Project from ₹100 crore to ₹150 crore, with approval from the parent company's Board. 

• 🏗️ The additional investment will be used to create an Integrated Aluminium Value Chain Unit, adding facilities for anodizing, machining, cutting, punching, assembly and other downstream processing to enhance value addition. 

• 📈 The revised project also includes a planned capacity expansion through the acquisition of additional adjacent land, along with investment in extra plant & machinery and related capital expenditure. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The higher investment expands manufacturing capabilities, increases value addition and supports long-term growth through enhanced aluminium processing capacity.)

PDS: DISSOLVES NON-OPERATIONAL US STEP-DOWN SUBSIDIARY 🏢📄

• 🏢 PDS has dissolved Design COE USA Inc., its step-down subsidiary in Delaware, USA, effective July 22, 2026, as part of its initiative to streamline the group's corporate structure by eliminating non-operational entities. 

• 📉 Following the dissolution, Design COE USA Inc. has ceased to be a step-down subsidiary of the company. 

• 📊 The company clarified that the subsidiary had no active business operations, with nil turnover and nil net worth, and therefore the transaction will have no material impact on PDS' financial position.  

🟡 Impact: Neutral (The dissolution is an internal corporate restructuring involving a non-operational subsidiary and has no material financial or operational impact.)

NTPC: COMMISSIONS 64.76 MW OF KHAVDA SOLAR PROJECT IN GUJARAT ☀️⚡

• 🚀 NTPC's step-down subsidiary NTPC Renewable Energy Ltd., through NTPC Green Energy Ltd. (NGEL), has declared the commercial operation (COD) of the first 64.76 MW out of the 225 MW GSECL RE Park, Khavda Solar PV Project in Gujarat, effective 24 July 2026. 

• 📈 With this commissioning, NTPC Group's total installed capacity has increased to 91,030 MW, while commercial capacity has reached 89,950 MW. 

• 🌱 At the subsidiary level, NTPC Green Energy Group's total installed capacity has increased to 10,786.56 MW following the commissioning. (As shown in the subsidiary disclosure on page 2.) 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The commissioning adds renewable generation capacity, strengthens NTPC's green energy portfolio and supports future renewable power generation and revenue growth.)

HCLTECH: TO SET UP ₹14,257 CRORE AI DATA CENTER IN ODISHA 🤖🏗️

• 🚀 HCLTech will establish its first AI Data Center in the upcoming Odisha Sovereign AI Park in partnership with Sarvam and the Government of Odisha, marking its entry into the full-stack AI infrastructure space. 

• 💰 The project involves a planned capital outlay of ₹14,257 crore, including financial assistance from the Government of Odisha. 

• 🧠 The AI Data Center will leverage HCLTech's full-stack AI capabilities and Sarvam's foundation models to deliver sector-specific AI solutions for public and private enterprises while strengthening India's sovereign AI ecosystem. 

• 🤝 An MoU has been signed between HCLTech, the Government of Odisha and Sarvam, with the company highlighting the project as a key milestone in its full-stack AI strategy. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The large-scale AI infrastructure investment strengthens HCLTech's AI strategy, expands its sovereign AI capabilities and creates significant long-term growth opportunities.)

TAC INFOSEC: TO ACQUIRE ISRAEL-BASED SAFEHOUSE TECHNOLOGIES FOR ₹1 CRORE 🌍🔐

• 🤝 TAC InfoSec has signed a binding term sheet to acquire 100% of Israel-based Safehouse Technologies Ltd., which will become its wholly owned subsidiary (or its business and IP will be transferred after due diligence). 

• 📱 Safehouse's flagship consumer cybersecurity app has 10 lakh+ downloads and a 4.2-star Google Play rating, offering VPN, encrypted browsing, phishing alerts, breach detection and privacy protection across multiple countries. 

• 💰 The proposed acquisition consideration is ₹1 crore, payable through a combination of cash and TAC InfoSec shares, and includes the acquisition of Safehouse's intellectual property, including source code, product designs, trademarks and know-how. 

• 🚀 The acquisition marks TAC InfoSec's strategic entry into the consumer (B2C) cybersecurity market, with plans to build TAC Safehouse Limited as a dedicated platform for consumer cybersecurity products and digital protection services.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition expands TAC InfoSec into the fast-growing consumer cybersecurity segment, adds an established product with a sizeable user base, and strengthens its long-term growth platform.)

• 📅 The dissolution became effective from July 21, 2026, and the company received intimation from Senseforth AI Research on July 23, 2026.

🟡 Impact: Neutral (While the order adds another customer and supports business relationships, the order value is very small and is unlikely to have a material financial impact.)

PETRONET LNG: DENIES REPORT ON QATARENERGY FORCE MAJEURE EXTENSION ⚠️🛢️

• 📢 Petronet LNG has clarified that it is not aware of QatarEnergy extending the Force Majeure through mid-October 2026, responding to stock exchange queries on a media report. 

• 📄 The company reiterated that it had already informed the exchanges on March 5, 2026 about the Force Majeure declared by QatarEnergy and the corresponding Force Majeure declared by Petronet LNG to GAIL, IOCL and BPCL under the relevant gas sale agreements. 

• 🌍 Petronet stated that the Force Majeure situation continues due to the ongoing hostilities in the region and blockage of the Strait of Hormuz. 

• 🔄 The company added that it has been taking adequate mitigation measures, including providing additional regasification capacity and slots, while the financial impact remains unquantifiable at this stage.  

🟡 Impact: Neutral (The clarification rules out any fresh information regarding an extension of the Force Majeure, while the existing situation and mitigation measures remain unchanged.)

GOVT RESTRICTS SUSPENSION-GRADE PVC RESIN IMPORTS 🇮🇳

• 🚫 Government has restricted imports of suspension-grade PVC resin for six months

• 💰 Restriction applies to imports with a delivered price above US$0.766/kg

• 📋 Move aims to regulate imports of suspension-grade PVC resin and support domestic supply

• 🏭 Domestic PVC manufacturers could benefit from reduced import competition during the restriction period

🟢 Likely Beneficiaries: Chemplast Sanmar, DCM Shriram, Finolex Industries (backward integration), Reliance Industries (PVC business)

🔴 Potentially Impacted: PVC pipe, cable and plastic processors dependent on imported PVC resin if domestic prices rise.

Impact: Positive for domestic PVC resin manufacturers; mixed for downstream PVC product manufacturers depending on raw material costs.

EPIGRAL is quietly repositioning itself beyond chlor-alkali

New subsidiary just floated

- Epigral Advanced Materials Limited incorporated on July 7, 2026

- 100% subsidiary of Epigral Limited

- Mandate: push into high-value downstream specialty chemistries across pharma, agrochemicals and electronics

This isn't a surprise if you read the FY26 annual report

- Company had already flagged a 100 acre land parcel at Dahej for what management called "new chemistry development"

- In the AR, they said this signals a growth plan beyond FY27-28, strictly downstream to existing derivatives

- They also said they'd share specifics only once project scope was finalized - this new entity looks like that formalization

Core capex cycle is still running in parallel

- CPVC Resin capacity being scaled from 75,000 TPA to 1,50,000 TPA

- Epichlorohydrin (ECH) capacity being doubled from 50,000 TPA to 1,00,000 TPA

- Both expected to commission by Q2 FY27

- Chlorotoluene value chain facility already commissioned but still in customer approval and ramp up phase, not yet showing up meaningfully in the P&L

- Consolidated capex in FY26 stood at ₹394 Cr, net debt/EBITDA comfortable at 0.9x

Where the new subsidiary fits

- Management has guided Derivative and Specialty products to reach ~70% of revenue by FY28

- The new subsidiary extends that shift beyond the existing basket into entirely new chemistries

- Core thesis is import substitution - cutting dependence on imported high end chemical products

- Balance sheet stays conservative enough (net debt/equity at 0.2x) to fund this without stretching leverage

FY26 was a soft year on realisations and volumes, but the direction of capital allocation hasn't changed. 

If anything, the new entity confirms the next leg of the growth story is already being built.

Waiting for the concall on 27th July for further details on the new entity.

HPCL: Guidance - - Concall Update

• Management generally avoids forward-looking guidance due to extreme market volatility, stating it is "the least time as a management team we would want to give forward-looking guidances."

• The annual CapEx target agreed with the Government of India was INR 9,700 crores, but management expects it to be lower if the current situation prevails, having spent INR 1,700 crores in Q1.

• For the Samriddhi 2.0 profitability improvement program, management has set an aggressive target of INR 1,500 crore on a run rate basis and aims for INR 1,000 crore in accruals for the current year.

• The Rajasthan Refinery is expected to ramp up to full capacity in Q3 FY '27, with petchem units coming online by the end of the financial year.

• Management anticipates the Vizag RUF unit to be fully stabilized in Q2 or Q3 FY '27, contributing significantly to margins and reducing dependence on third-party product sourcing.

Bansal Wire Industries: Guidance -- Concall Update

• Management is targeting a 20% volume growth for the remainder of the year, supported by market share gains, specialty portfolio contributions, and sufficient installed capacity.

• The company expects EBITDA to grow by at least 20%, aligning with volume growth, despite starting the quarter with a higher EBITDA base.

• For IHT/OHT wires, management aims for 50% capacity utilization next month, reaching optimum utilization within the year, specifically 60-80% by year-end for IHT.

• The B2C segment's ambition is to contribute 50% of the low-carbon business volume, equating to 25% of total sales, with 20-30% higher EBITDA per ton compared to B2B low-carbon.

• The company is confident in achieving INR800 crore in free cash flow from operating activities combined for FY27 and FY28, an increase from the previous INR600 crore guidance.

*Bernstein on Bajaj Auto*
O-p, TP Rs 11500
Delivered a strong quarter.

Would also highlight Bajaj's earnings call as a benchmark for disclosure quality.
Management was transparent on strategy, commodity inflation, production disruptions, & path from margin pressure to EBITDA recovery, providing a level of detail that remains
best-in-class among 2W OEMs.

O-p TP Rs 12068
1QFY27 Ebitda margin of 20.9% (+9bps QoQ and 69bps ahead of estimate), despite commodity cost inflation of 4.5% QoQ
Impact of higher raw material costs was largely offset by
(1) rupee depreciation given 40% of revenue is derived from export markets,
(2) 2.3% price increase,
(3) operating leverage,
(4) rich product mix
5) discretionary cost-control measures

Raise FY27-29E EPS by 6-8%

*JPM on Bandhan BK*
Neutral, TP Rs 175
Guidance cut was key focus in Bandhan’s 1QFY27 earnings call with a significant cut in FY27 RoA and growth targets.
Bandhan has cut 4QFY27 RoA guidance by 40bps to 1.2-1.4% (vs 1.6-1.8% earlier) driven primarily by external factors:
(i) 30bps RoA impact from moderation in NIM outlook (from 6.5% to 1Q level of 6.2%) on account of tighter liquidity and elevated funding costs, and
(ii) 10bps RoA impact from elevated opex especially rising technology related costs (from 4.2% to 1Q level of 4.3%).
Also, FY27 loan growth guidance was cut to lower end of the 14-15% range guided earlier with a conscious growth slowdown owing to rising AQ risk from higher energy costs and supply chain disruptions as well as uncertain monsoons.

*Nomura on Bandhan BK*
Neutral, TP Rs 190
1QFY27 PAT of INR5bn (+35% y-y, -6% q-q) beat estimate by 8%, driven by strong NII, fee income and lower credit costs.
The real story, though, is management cutting FY27F RoA guidance to 1.2-1.4% (from 1.6-1.8%), citing cost-offunds pressure & rising tech opex tied to external environment

Of 40bp cut, 30bp reflects NIM expansion that management had earlier expected over the coming quarters but no longer sees playing out, & 10bp reflects higher tech-related opex
Bank raised average SA rates by 20bp and peak TD rates by 20bp towards the end of 1Q, on intense deposit competition, so cost impact will flow through over coming quarters
This comes as a surprise given had expected funding costs to ease on RBI's supportive measures.
Asset quality held up: credit costs at 1.8% (down slightly q-q), though gross slippages ticked up to 2.9% (vs 2.8% in 4QFY26)
Cut FY27-28F EPS by 4-7%, building in 18-24bp lower NIMs even after lowering credit cost assumptions to 1.8% (from 2%).

*CLSA on Bandhan Bk*
O-P, TP Rs 235
A decent quarter but a cautious management outlook
Reported PAT of Rs5bn vs estimate of Rs5.3bn.
While NII beat estimate by 4%, lower treasury gains and higher Opex led to a PAT miss.
Gross loan growth optically accelerated to 16% due to a low YoY base.
Reported NIM was stable at 6.2% QoQ.
Asset quality was strong with the net slippage ratio declining 20bps QoQ to 1.7% (last delivered in 1QFY25).
Management cut its FY27 exit ROA target from 1.6%-1.8% earlier to 1.2%-1.4% now citing higher funding costs & a challenging external environment
While this may spook street, our estimate was lower anyway (FY28 ROA of 1.3%).

Financial Performance

Order Wins

Strategic Initiatives

Impact

- BEV business growing rapidly.
- Strong order inflows.
- Robotics adds new growth avenue.
- Diversification strengthens outlook.

IndiGo | Q1 FY27 Results

Financial Performance
- Total income ₹25,614 crore (+18.9% YoY).
- Revenue ₹24,584 crore (+19.9% YoY).
- Ticket revenue ₹21,879 crore (+23.0% YoY).
- Ancillary revenue ₹2,453 crore (+13.9% YoY).
- EBITDAR ₹3,833 crore (-33.2% YoY).
- EBITDAR margin 15.6%.
- Reported PAT loss of ₹238 crore.
- Ex-forex PAT loss ₹6 crore.

Operational Highlights
- Capacity increased 2.9% YoY.
- Carried 31.3 million passengers.
- Yield rose 21.3% YoY.
- RASK increased 16.5% YoY.
- Load factor at 83.3%.

- Peak 2,298 daily flights operated.
- Network covers 143 destinations.
- Technical dispatch reliability 99.9%.
- On-time performance 86.9%.

Cost & Balance Sheet
- Total expenses rose 34.4% YoY.
- Fuel cost surged 85.7% YoY.
- CASK increased 32.6% YoY.
- Cash balance at ₹52,885 crore.
- Free cash at ₹39,039 crore.
- Total debt at ₹81,531 crore.

Management Commentary
- Profitability hit by fuel prices.
- Rupee depreciation impacted costs.
- West Asia disruptions affected operations.
- Passenger demand remained healthy.
- Higher yields supported revenue.
- Capacity to remain broadly flat in Q2.
- Aircraft utilization expected to improve.

Impact
- Neutral.
- Revenue growth remained strong.
- Margins impacted by higher costs.
- Loss driven by fuel and forex.
- Strong liquidity provides resilience.
- Demand outlook remains healthy.

Key Takeaway
- IndiGo delivered strong revenue growth in Q1 FY27, supported by higher fares and resilient passenger demand. However, a sharp rise in fuel costs, rupee depreciation and Middle East-related disruptions led to a quarterly loss and margin pressure. Despite near-term challenges, the airline remains well positioned with a strong balance sheet, expanding fleet and healthy demand outlook.

Financial Performance
- AUM at ₹4,887 crore (-1% YoY, +11% QoQ).

Operational Highlights

- PAT and AUM improved sequentially.
- 61% of disbursements to new customers.

Impact

- Profitability returned.
- Asset quality improved.
- Collection efficiency strengthened.
- Growth momentum recovering.
- Strong capital supports expansion.

Key Takeaway
- Spandana Sphoorty delivered a strong turnaround in Q1 FY27 with a return to profitability, improving asset quality, higher collection efficiency and double-digit sequential AUM growth. Management believes the company has entered a recovery phase and is well positioned for sustainable growth backed by strong capital, liquidity and multiple business expansion initiatives.

ACME SOLAR: OPERATIONALIZES 3.62 GWH BESS CAPACITY IN RAJASTHAN ⚡🔋

• 🔋 Operational BESS capacity reaches ~3.62 GWh across 3 project sites in Rajasthan, among the fastest BESS scale-ups in India

• ⚡ Commissioned ~2.29 GWh of BESS capacity in the current financial year till date

• 🌞 Milestone strengthens grid stability, supports peak-hour demand and enables round-the-clock renewable power

• 🔥 RCA on the Pokhran BESS incident confirms an electrical short circuit caused a localized AC cable fire; no equipment damage and restoration has been completed

• 🏗️ Operational contracted portfolio: 2,990 MW + ~3.62 GWh BESS; under-construction portfolio: 5,080 MW

🟢 Impact: Positive (Higher operational BESS capacity strengthens ACME's renewable energy portfolio and execution track record.)

Allied Blenders & Distillers | Q1FY27 Highlights

Financial Highlights (Consolidated)
- Revenue ₹984 crore (+5.8% YoY).
- EBITDA ₹120 crore (+1.2% YoY).
- EBITDA margin 12.2% (-60 bps YoY).
- PAT ₹45 crore (-18.7% YoY).
- One-off supply disruption impacted profits.

Adjusted Performance
- Adjusted EBITDA ₹144 crore (+21.4% YoY).
- Adjusted EBITDA margin 14.7% (+189 bps).
- Adjusted PAT ₹63 crore (+13.6% YoY).

Business Performance
- Revenue driven by premiumisation.
- Product mix continued improving.
- Volumes increased 6.2% YoY.
- Total volumes reached 9 million cases.
- P&A volumes grew 10.7% YoY.
- Mass Premium volumes grew 2.3% YoY.
- P&A value share rose to 59.3%.

Brand & Expansion
- ICONiQ White sold 3.1 million cases.
- ICONiQ White grew 33.8% YoY.
- Launched Zoya Pink.
- Presence expanded to 39 countries.
- ICONiQ entered 10 new markets.
- ABD Maestro expanded globally.

Management Outlook
- Premiumisation strategy progressing well.
- Investments continue in brands and distribution.
- India-UK FTA expected to boost margins.
- Medium-term topline growth targeted at mid-teens.

Impact
- Positive.
- Premium portfolio gaining momentum.
- International expansion accelerating.
- Temporary supply disruptions remain monitorable.

Key Takeaway
- Allied Blenders delivered another quarter of strong premiumisation-led growth, with higher volumes, improving product mix and rapid global expansion. While one-off supply chain disruptions affected reported profitability, the underlying business remains strong and management continues to target mid-teen revenue growth supported by premium brands and the India-UK FTA.

KRSNAA DIAGNOSTICS: EXPANDS HIMACHAL PRADESH PPP CT SCAN PROJECT TO 34 CENTRES 🏥📈

• 🤝 Krsnaa Diagnostics has signed a Public Private Partnership (PPP) agreement with the Health & Family Welfare Department, Government of Himachal Pradesh to set up, operate and manage CT Scan diagnostic facilities at 34 Government Health Institutions across the state. 

• 📈 The project scope has been expanded from 12 to 34 Government Health Institutions, making it one of the company's largest government partnerships for CT scan diagnostic services. 

• 📅 The project will run on a PPP model for an initial 7 years, with an option to extend by another 3 years, and will operate on a majority cash basis. 

• 💰 The company stated that the contract value cannot currently be estimated, as it is a new project. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The significant expansion in project scope strengthens Krsnaa Diagnostics' government healthcare portfolio, improves long-term revenue visibility and reinforces its leadership in PPP diagnostics.)

BELDING INDIA: ACQUIRES 100% STAKE IN EVOLVE IT SOLUTIONS 🚀🛡️

• 🤝 Belding India has acquired a 100% equity stake in Evolve IT Solutions Pvt. Ltd., strengthening its presence in advanced manufacturing and security technologies. 

• 🛡️ Evolve specializes in advanced X-ray inspection and security screening solutions, including baggage scanners, cargo inspection systems, vehicle scanners, under-vehicle surveillance systems and dual-view X-ray systems, serving airports, ports, defence, metro rail and other critical infrastructure. 

• ⚙️ The acquisition expands Belding's capabilities in AI-enabled imaging, industrial electronics, precision engineering and mission-critical security technologies, creating opportunities in both domestic and international markets. 

• 🇮🇳 The company said the acquisition supports its strategy to build an indigenous engineering and technology platform, while promoting Make in India, import substitution and long-term growth. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The acquisition broadens Belding's technology portfolio, strengthens its defence and security offerings, and enhances long-term growth prospects.)

• ⚡ The order is for the domestic market and covers the supply of cables & conductors. 

• 📅 The company expects to complete delivery by September 2026, providing near-term revenue visibility.

🟢 Impact: Positive (The ₹18 crore order strengthens the company's order book and supports revenue growth over the coming months.)

HARIOM PIPE: CRISIL REVISES RATING OUTLOOK TO 'STABLE' 📈🏦

• ⭐ CRISIL Ratings has revised the outlook on Hariom Pipe Industries' long-term bank facilities from 'CRISIL A-/Watch Developing' to 'CRISIL A-/Stable'. 

• ✅ The short-term non-fund based rating has also been revised from 'CRISIL A2+/Watch Developing' to 'CRISIL A2+', with the 'Watch Developing' status removed. 

• 🏦 The revised ratings apply to the company's fund-based long-term and non-fund based short-term banking facilities. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Removal of the 'Watch Developing' status and assignment of a stable outlook reflect improved credit profile and enhance the company's credibility with lenders.)

Key takeaways from Nandan Kulkarni interview on the potential impact of US pharma tariffs.

While the market is focused on tariff headlines, Nandan argues that the bigger story is how tariffs could reshape the economics of the US generic drug industry.

Here are the insights that stood out to us 

1. The generic pricing model may finally reset
 - According to Nandan, US generic drug prices have been under pressure for years. If manufacturing shifts back to the US, the current pricing simply won't support the economics.
 - Higher labour costs, operating expenses, FDA site transfers, and fresh capital investment all require higher Average Selling Prices (ASPs) to make domestic manufacturing viable.
 - The industry may be entering a structural pricing reset rather than facing just another policy headwind.

2. Drug shortages could become a major catalyst
- One interesting point he highlighted is that shortages in several acute drug categories have already started appearing in the US.
 - Any attempt to accelerate onshoring could tighten supply further before new capacity comes online.
 - That potentially creates:
 • Better pricing
 • Improved profitability
 • Stronger negotiating power for reliable manufacturers
Sometimes the second-order effects matter more than the headline itself.

3. Existing US manufacturing becomes a competitive advantage
Not every company starts from the same position.
Nandan believes companies that already have manufacturing operations in the US—such as Sun Pharma, Lupin and Zydus Lifesciences—could adapt much faster if localisation gathers pace. Rather than building entirely new facilities, these companies may be able to expand existing sites and execute product transfers more efficiently.

4. The market may be underestimating the timeline
A key observation was that investors shouldn't wait for tariffs to be formally implemented.
The next 12–18 months could already see:
 • Pricing negotiations with PBMs
 • Strategic inventory build-up
 • Manufacturing transfer planning
 • Incremental US capex decisions
Markets often discount these developments well before policy becomes effective.

5. Execution—not tariffs—will separate winners
 - An important distinction Nandan made was between simple oral generics and complex biologics.
 - Traditional tablets and capsules are relatively easier to relocate.
 - Biosimilars and other complex products involve significantly longer technology transfers and regulatory approvals, making the transition far more challenging.

6. Better pricing could fund future growth
 - One concern investors have is whether companies will need massive balance sheet investments.
 - Nandan's argument is interesting: if generic prices improve because of tighter supply and higher manufacturing costs, part of the incremental profitability itself could finance future US manufacturing investments.
 - That changes the capital allocation equation.

7. The long-term opportunity extends beyond generics
Perhaps the most important takeaway was his long-term view.
Rather than remaining low-cost manufacturers, Indian pharma companies could accelerate investments in:
 - Complex generics
 - 505(b)(2) products
 - Specialty pharmaceuticals
 - Orphan drugs
 - Peptides
 - Innovation-led portfolios
If that transition plays out, tariffs may ultimately become a catalyst for higher-value pharmaceutical businesses rather than simply a near-term disruption.

Our takeaway
The discussion shifts the focus away from "Will tariffs hurt Indian pharma?" to a more important question:
Could tariffs accelerate a healthier industry structure with better pricing, stronger margins and greater innovation?

Infosys | Q1 FY27 Results

Financial Highlights
- Revenue ₹48,211 crore (+14% YoY, +4% QoQ).
- EBIT ₹10,163 crore (+30% YoY, +17% QoQ).
- EBIT Margin 21.1% (vs 20.8% YoY).
- PAT ₹7,769 crore (+12% YoY, -9% QoQ).
- CC Revenue Growth +1% QoQ.
- Dollar Revenue US$5.08 billion (+1% QoQ).

Deal Wins & Business Momentum
- TCV increased to US$3.6 billion (+13% QoQ).
- Strong large deal wins across segments.
- AI momentum translating into revenue.
- Continued market share gains.
- Infosys Topaz driving enterprise AI adoption.

Management Commentary
- AI demand gaining traction.
- Strategic partnerships with leading AI companies.
- Focus on employee AI reskilling.
- Continued investments in AI, talent and platforms.
- Improving productivity and operating leverage.

Guidance
- FY27 revenue growth guidance revised to 1.5%–3.0%.
- Earlier guidance was 1.5%–3.5%.
- Reflects a more cautious growth outlook.

Leadership Transition
- Ashiss Kumar Dash appointed CEO & MD-designate.
- To succeed Salil Parekh from 1 April 2027.
- Over 31 years with Infosys.
- Expected to lead the company's next AI-led growth phase.

Impact
- Neutral.
- Strong execution and deal pipeline.
- AI business continues to strengthen.
- Guidance cut may weigh on near-term sentiment.
- Leadership transition appears well planned.

Key Takeaway
- Infosys delivered healthy Q1 FY27 growth with stable margins, robust deal wins and increasing AI-led revenue momentum. However, the reduction in FY27 revenue guidance indicates a more cautious demand environment. While near-term sentiment may remain muted, the strong order book, continued AI investments and a well-planned leadership transition position the company well for long-term growth.

INFOSYS Q1 : Board approved its intention to appoint Ashiss Kumar Dash as the Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director of the Company with effect from April 1, 2027, upon fulfilment of all statutory requirements.

Jubilant Ingrevia | Q1 FY27 Results

Financial Highlights
- Revenue ₹1,300 crore (+25% YoY, +10% QoQ).
- EBITDA ₹209 crore (+36% YoY, +22% QoQ).
- EBITDA Margin 16% (vs 15% last year).
- PAT ₹106 crore (+41% YoY, +22% QoQ).
- EPS ₹6.7 (+41% YoY, +22% QoQ).

Business Performance
- Strong performance across all business segments.
- Broad-based revenue and profit growth.
- Healthy margin expansion.
- Better operational execution.

Key Monitorables
- Progress of the US$300 million agrochemical project.
- Customer execution and project timelines.
- Sustainability of volume growth.
- Contribution from pricing versus volumes.
- Management commentary on demand outlook.

Long-Term Growth Drivers
- Execution of the Pinnacle Strategy.
- Higher contribution from Specialty Chemicals.
- Expansion of CDMO business.
- Improving business mix towards value-added segments.
- Potential for stronger long-term profitability.

Impact
- Positive.
- Strong quarterly performance.
- Margin improvement continues.
- Execution remains the key value driver.
- Management commentary will determine earnings sustainability.

Key Takeaway
- Jubilant Ingrevia delivered a strong Q1 FY27 with healthy growth in revenue, profitability and margins across all business segments. While the quarterly performance is encouraging, the long-term investment case continues to depend on successful execution of the Pinnacle Strategy, progress on the US$300 million agrochemical project and the company's ability to increase the share of higher-margin Specialty Chemicals and CDMO businesses.

PTC Industries has received a design & development order from ARDE (DRDO) for a Titanium Cradle used in the Indian Light Weight Tank programme.

Moving up the value chain! 

While the order value hasn't been disclosed due to the strategic nature of the project, the announcement is noteworthy for another reason.

Unlike conventional manufacturing contracts, this assignment requires PTC to design, develop, validate and manufacture a mission-critical titanium component.

This indicates a shift from executing customer-provided designs to participating earlier in the product development cycle, where engineering capability, material expertise and validation become equally important.

Titanium is increasingly being used in defence applications because of its high strength-to-weight ratio, corrosion resistance and durability, making it suitable for lightweight platforms such as the Indian Light Weight Tank.

The execution timeline for the project is 2.5 years, and further developments would depend on successful design validation and subsequent programme requirements.

**JSW Energy | Brokerage Update (CLSA)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Hold.
- Target Price: ₹549.

**Q1 Assessment**
- PAT declined 37% YoY.
- Weak quarter despite thermal acquisition.
- Higher funding costs hurt earnings.
- Lower PPA tariffs impacted profitability.
- Weak generation affected merchant business.

**Brokerage View**
- Merchant power exposure reducing.
- Coal asset additions slowing transition.
- Cuts FY27–FY29 EPS by 11–13%.
- Valuation remains expensive.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA maintains a Hold rating on JSW Energy, citing weak earnings, rising leverage costs, tariff pressure, and slower clean-energy transition despite ongoing capacity expansion.**

**JSW Energy | Brokerage Update (Jefferies)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy.
- Target Price: ₹720.

**Q1 Assessment**
- EBITDA beat estimates by 7%.
- Higher capacity additions supported earnings.
- EBITDA grew 3% YoY.
- KSK plant shutdown impacted performance.

**Growth Outlook**
- KSK operations have stabilized.
- Added 1.1 GW capacity so far.
- On track for 2.2 GW FY27 target.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies maintains a Buy rating on JSW Energy, citing strong capacity additions and execution, with temporary operational disruptions already stabilizing and keeping the company on track for its FY27 expansion plans.**

**Nestlé India | Brokerage Update (CLSA)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Outperform.
- Target Price: ₹1,638.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Sales grew 25.4% YoY.
- Revenue beat CLSA and consensus.
- PAT jumped 49% YoY.
- All segments posted double-digit growth.
- EBITDA margin beat expectations.
- Gross profit saw strongest growth since 3QCY16.

**Growth Drivers**
- Volume-led growth.
- Premiumisation accelerated.
- Quick Commerce boosted premium mix.
- Higher ad spends absorbed well.

**Brokerage View**
- Raises FY27–FY29 earnings by 4–7%.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA reiterates an Outperform rating on Nestlé India, driven by broad-based volume growth, accelerating premiumisation, and strong margin expansion, prompting an upgrade to its earnings forecasts.**

**Nestlé India | Brokerage Update (Citi)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy.
- Target Price: ₹1,750 (raised).

**Q1 Assessment**
- Third straight beat-and-raise quarter.
- Revenue, EBITDA and PAT grew strongly.
- Broad-based double-digit segment growth.
- Performance driven by volume growth.
- EBITDA margin expanded to 24.2%.

**Growth Drivers**
- Rural distribution expansion.
- Strong e-commerce momentum.
- Faster premiumisation.
- Higher A&P investments.
- Gross margin and operating leverage improved.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi reiterates a Buy rating on Nestlé India and raises its target price to ₹1,750, citing broad-based volume-led growth, sustained market share gains, and margin expansion despite significantly higher brand investments.**
Mirae Asset Sharekhan Morning Tiger -38.pdf

**Nestlé India | Brokerage Update (Nomura)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy.
- Target Price: ₹1,675.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Third consecutive standout quarter.
- Beat estimates across all metrics.
- Volume mix rose 20% YoY.
- Sales grew 25% YoY.
- Gross and operating margins expanded.
- Operating profit surged 40% YoY.

**Brokerage View**
- Strong performance despite higher A&P spends.
- Growth likely moderates from Q2/Q3 FY27.
- High base effect to normalize growth.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura reiterates a Buy rating on Nestlé India, highlighting another strong earnings beat driven by robust volume-led growth and margin expansion, while expecting reported growth to normalize as the base becomes more demanding.**

**Nestlé India | Brokerage Update (HSBC)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Hold.
- Target Price: ₹1,480.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Revenue grew 25% YoY.
- Beat consensus and HSBC estimates.
- Double-digit growth across segments.
- Milk Nutrition growth revived.

**Brokerage View**
- Milk Nutrition recovery on a favorable base.
- Sustainability of growth remains uncertain.
- Retains premium 60x forward P/E.

**Key Takeaway**
- **HSBC maintains a Hold rating on Nestlé India, acknowledging strong revenue growth and broad-based demand while remaining cautious on the sustainability of Milk Nutrition recovery and retaining its premium valuation stance.**

**Eternal | Brokerage Update (HSBC)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy.
- Target Price: ₹340.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Food Delivery grew strongly.
- Quick Commerce remained robust.
- Margins improved with operating leverage.

**Brokerage View**
- Q1 supports FY27 estimates.
- Competition appears stabilizing.
- Expects sustained earnings growth.
- Sees 15–20% stock CAGR potential.

**Key Takeaway**
- **HSBC maintains a Buy rating on Eternal, citing strong execution across Food Delivery and Quick Commerce, improving profitability, and confidence in long-term compounding as competitive intensity stabilizes.**

**Eternal | Brokerage Update (Nomura)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy.
- Target Price: ₹350.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Food Delivery growth accelerated.
- Quick Commerce rebounded strongly.
- Blinkit profitability improved further.

**Management Outlook**
- Raises steady-state EBITDA margin target to 6%.
- Focus on larger stores and warehouses.
- Expanding assortments and efficiency.
- Better working capital management.

**Brokerage View**
- Expects 57–74% NOV growth in FY27–28.
- Forecasts continued EBITDA margin improvement.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura maintains a Buy rating on Eternal, driven by accelerating growth across Food Delivery and Blinkit, with improving profitability supported by operational efficiencies and scale.**

**Eternal | Brokerage Update (Jefferies)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy.
- Target Price: ₹415.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Growth remained high quality.
- Food Delivery outperformed expectations.
- Quick Commerce delivered healthy growth.
- Profitability stayed resilient.

**Management Outlook**
- Competition becoming predictable.
- Value-led Food Delivery seen unsustainable.
- Blinkit avoids aggressive discounting.
- Prioritizes profitable long-term growth.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies reiterates a Buy rating on Eternal, highlighting disciplined execution, resilient profitability, and management's focus on sustainable growth over market-share gains.**

**Eternal | Brokerage Update (CLSA)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: High Conviction Outperform.
- Target Price: ₹506.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Strong execution across businesses.
- Quick Commerce growth accelerated.
- Food Delivery growth gained momentum.
- Profitability improved despite competition.
- Blinkit boosted profitability confidence.

**Growth Drivers**
- Zomato posted fastest growth in six quarters.
- Limited impact from no-commission platforms.
- District and Bistro expanded ecosystem.
- New businesses increased engagement.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA reiterates its High Conviction Outperform rating on Eternal, highlighting strong execution, accelerating growth across core businesses, and rising confidence in Blinkit's profitability despite an intensely competitive market.**

**Eternal | Brokerage Update (JPMorgan)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Overweight.
- Target Price: ₹390.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Results were strong and in line.
- Quick Commerce growth accelerated.
- Blinkit customer additions remained robust.
- QC EBITDA turned positive despite competition.
- Food Delivery growth exceeded estimates.

**Management Outlook**
- Competition likely at peak.
- Confident in handling competitive actions.
- Raises long-term EBITDA margin target to 6%.
- Increases capex per store to ₹25 million.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan maintains an Overweight rating on Eternal, citing resilient execution across Blinkit and Food Delivery, improving profitability, and higher long-term margin ambitions despite intense competition.**

**United Spirits | Brokerage Update (CLSA)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Hold.
- Target Price: ₹1,285.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Sales grew 6% YoY.
- Revenue was in line.
- Gross margin expanded 212 bps YoY.
- EBITDA margin contracted 30 bps YoY.
- Higher ad spending hurt margins.
- EBITDA missed estimates.

**Brokerage View**
- Margin below CLSA expectations.
- EBITDA below CLSA and consensus.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA maintains a Hold rating on United Spirits as healthy sales growth was offset by elevated advertising spends, leading to weaker-than-expected margins and EBITDA.**

**United Spirits | Brokerage Update (Nomura)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy.
- Target Price: ₹1,500.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Sales met estimates.
- EBITDA missed expectations.
- Premium and Popular volumes declined.
- Gross margin contracted sequentially.
- Higher A&P spend hurt profitability.

**Outlook**
- Expects 1H FY27 headwinds.
- Volume and margin pressure to persist.
- 2H FY27 supported by India-UK FTA.
- MML impact to normalize.
- Softer glass prices to aid margins.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura maintains a Buy rating on United Spirits, expecting near-term pressure to ease in the second half of FY27 as cost headwinds moderate and structural tailwinds improve margins and growth.**

**Dr. Reddy's Laboratories | Brokerage Update (Jefferies)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Underperform.
- Target Price: ₹1,040.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Q1 materially missed estimates.
- Margins hurt by weak US business.
- Higher raw material and SG&A costs.
- India, Russia and Europe grew strongly.

**Management Outlook**
- Expects better margins in 2H FY27.
- Semaglutide delays impacted FY27 outlook.
- Abatacept approval expected by Dec-2026.

**Brokerage View**
- Cuts FY27–FY29 estimates.
- Sees risks from slower Sema ramp-up.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies retains an Underperform rating on Dr. Reddy's, citing weaker-than-expected profitability and delays in key growth drivers, while expecting margin recovery only in the second half of FY27.**

**Dr. Reddy's Laboratories | Brokerage Update (JPMorgan)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Underweight.
- Target Price: ₹1,100.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Adjusted revenue, EBITDA and PAT declined YoY.
- EBITDA margin at 14.3%.
- Higher SG&A and freight costs hurt margins.
- Core profitability remained weak.

**Management Outlook**
- Semaglutide supply expected by Nov-2026.
- Abatacept approval targeted in early 2027.
- Sees 18–19% EBITDA margin by FY28–29.

**Brokerage View**
- Delays could impact earnings.
- Base business valuation remains expensive.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan retains an Underweight rating on Dr. Reddy's, citing structurally weak core profitability and rich valuations, while noting that the expected recovery depends heavily on timely execution of the Semaglutide and Abatacept opportunities.**

**Dr. Reddy's Laboratories | Brokerage Update (CLSA)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Hold.
- Target Price: ₹1,240.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Revenue met estimates.
- Margin missed due to gSema issues.
- Higher solvent and freight costs impacted margins.

**Management Outlook**
- gSema issues expected to stabilize by Nov-2026.
- Guides double-digit US base business growth.
- Targets ~20% FY27 EBITDA margin (ex-gSema).
- Expects US generic tariffs unlikely.

**Brokerage View**
- Cuts FY27 revenue estimate.
- Moderates FY27–FY29 margin estimates.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA maintains a Hold rating on Dr. Reddy's as temporary gSema-related disruptions weigh on FY27 earnings, while management remains confident of a recovery through stronger US growth and margin normalization.**
**Indo-MIM -** IPO Note

**Dr. Reddy's Laboratories | Brokerage Update (Citi)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Sell.
- Target Price: ₹1,040.

**Q1 Assessment**
- EBITDA margin fell to 13.3%.
- Gross margin declined to 49.5%.
- Core profitability at multi-year lows.
- US sales weakened despite Bosutinib launch.

**Key Highlights**
- Base US business remained weak.
- Ex-US growth stayed investment-led.
- SG&A rose 12% YoY.

**Management Outlook**
- Semaglutide supply by Nov-2026.
- Abatacept approval expected by Dec-2026.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi remains cautious on Dr. Reddy's due to weak core profitability and US business pressure, despite expected second-half recovery from semaglutide supply normalization and potential new product approvals.**

**IndusInd Bank | Brokerage Update (JPMorgan)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Underweight.
- Target Price: ₹820 (Raised).

**Q1 Assessment**
- PAT beat estimates.
- Reported NII and PPoP exceeded expectations.
- Core NII broadly in line.
- Core NIM declined 4 bps QoQ.
- Asset quality improved with lower slippages.

**Management Outlook**
- Targets industry-level FY27 loan growth.
- Aims for 1% exit RoA.
- Operating leverage and lower credit costs to drive recovery.

**Brokerage View**
- Stock up 23% in three months.
- Current valuation seen as pricing in recovery.
- Risk-reward viewed as unfavorable.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan acknowledges improving operating performance and asset quality at IndusInd Bank but retains an Underweight rating, believing the recent rally already reflects the expected recovery and limits further upside.**

**IndusInd Bank | Brokerage Update (Citi)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Sell.
- Target Price: ₹990 (Raised).

**Q1 Assessment**
- Core RoA improved to 0.63%.
- Opex and credit costs supported earnings.
- IT refund interest lifted reported RoA to 0.78%.
- Core NIM declined, offset by higher fee income.

**Business Trends**
- Loan growth led by wholesale segment.
- Retail and SME growth remained weak.
- Management sees Q1 as an inflection point.

**Outlook**
- Targets 1% RoA by FY27-end.
- Earnings estimates raised for FY27 and FY28.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi raised its earnings estimates and target price for IndusInd Bank after a stronger-than-expected Q1, but retains a Sell rating due to concerns over the quality of loan growth and the pace of a sustainable profitability recovery.**

**IndusInd Bank | Brokerage Update (CLSA)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Underperform (Downgraded).
- Target Price: ₹925 (Raised).

**Q1 Assessment**
- Core NII met estimates.
- Lower opex and credit costs lifted core PBT.
- Retail deposit growth improved.
- MFI-led slippages declined sharply.

**Key Concerns**
- Loan growth driven by corporate segment.
- Core NIM softened.
- Fee income remained weak.

**Outlook**
- Management guides industry-level FY27 loan growth.
- Targets 1% exit ROA in FY27.

**Brokerage View**
- Recent 17% stock rally seen as unwarranted.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA downgraded IndusInd Bank to Underperform despite raising its target price, believing recent share price gains have outpaced fundamentals, even as operational trends show gradual improvement.**

**IndusInd Bank | Brokerage Update (Jefferies)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy.
- Target Price: ₹1,250 (Raised).

**Q1 Assessment**
- Profit beat estimates.
- Growth trends improving.
- Asset quality strengthened.
- Core ROA improved.
- Lower opex and credit costs supported earnings.

**Outlook**
- Improvement expected over next 2–3 years.
- Driven by new management team.
- Expects 1.1% ROA by FY29.

**Valuation**
- Values stock at 1.3x FY27 adjusted P/B.
- 1.5% ROA seen as rerating trigger.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains positive on IndusInd Bank, citing improving earnings quality, strengthening asset quality, and a gradual ROA recovery, with sustained profitability improvement seen as the key catalyst for valuation rerating.**

**Sona BLW Precision Forgings | Brokerage Update (Jefferies)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy.
- Target Price: ₹700.

**Brokerage View**
- DENSO partnership broadens technology capabilities.
- Creates new growth opportunities in 4Ws.
- Timely amid rising EV adoption.
- Positive for 2W and 4W segments.

**Key Monitor**
- Transaction structure.
- Strategic rationale during concall.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains positive on Sona BLW, viewing its partnership with DENSO as a timely strategic move that enhances technology capabilities and strengthens long-term growth opportunities in India's expanding EV market.**

**Sona BLW Precision Forgings | Brokerage & Business Update**

**Brokerage View**
- Citi: Buy.
- Target Price: ₹700.

**Strategic Partnership**
- Partners with DENSO.
- Forms two EV powertrain JVs.

**JV Structure**
- 4W JV: DENSO 51%, Sona 49%.
- Makes traction inverters, motors and generators.
- 2W/3W JV: Sona 51%, DENSO 49%.
- DENSO invests at ₹1,750 Cr EV.

**Strategic Impact**
- Strengthens 4W EV presence.
- Expands hybrid and EV capabilities.
- Gains DENSO's technology expertise.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi believes the DENSO partnership strengthens Sona BLW's EV powertrain capabilities, particularly in four-wheelers, while enhancing its long-term technology and growth prospects.**

STEEL EXCHANGE INDIA LIMITED REPORTED STRONG Q1 FY27 PERFORMANCE WITH NET PROFIT RISING ~47% YOY TO ~₹1503 LAKHS

📌 Q1 FY27 STANDALONE KEY FINANCIAL HIGHLIGHTS

Total Income of ₹27,070.92 Lakhs

Net Profit of ₹1,502.56 Lakhs, YoY growth of 46.89%

Net Profit Margin of 6.00%, improved by 220 Bps YoY

Basic & Diluted EPS of ₹0.12, YoY growth of 33.33%

Debt-to-Equity Ratio improved to 0.27x from 0.48x YoY

KEY FINANCIAL & OPERATIONAL HIGHLIGHTS (Q1 FY27 VS Q1 FY26)

Net Profit Skyrockets by 46.89%: Stood at ₹1,502.56 Lakhs (₹15.03 Cr) for Q1 FY27, up from ₹1,022.88 Lakhs in Q1 FY26

Net Profit Margin Doubles YoY: Scaled to 6.00% in Q1 FY27 compared to 3.00% in Q1 FY26

Dramatic Debt Reduction: Total Debt-to-Equity Ratio dropped to 0.27x from 0.48x

Massive Net Worth Expansion: Corporate Net Worth grew to ₹67,276.25 Lakhs (₹672.76 Cr)

Enhanced Asset Coverage & Liquidity: Asset Coverage Ratio improved to 3.77x (vs 2.91x), Current Ratio at 2.57x

Jefferies in IndiaMart
U-P, TP Rs 1650

Q1 revs were in line while margins/profits were ahead.

Decline in paid supplier base for third straight quarter was a key negative surprise.

Margin expansion was driven by lower customer acquisition costs, which should normalize once the gross addition picks up.

Continued weakness in sub adds can disrupt network effects historically enjoyed by the platform

Cut FY28-29E EPS by ~1.5-4%

Nomura on IndiaMart
Reduce, TP Rs 1810

Paying subscriber addition woes continue – reduction continued in 1Q

Product evolution critical to improve subscriber addition in medium to long term

Believe subscriber addition has to turn meaningfully positive for stock to rise significantly from here

Value Indiamart’s core business at 18x P/E and continue to assign a 1x book value to its recent investments.

**Nykaa | Brokerage Update (HSBC)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy (Upgraded).
- Target Price: ₹380.

**Investment Thesis**
- Sees Nykaa as online BPC leader.
- Gaining market share in fashion.
- Strong long-term growth visibility.

**Earnings Outlook**
- Raises FY28 EBITDA estimate by 12%.
- Driven by stronger BPC margins.
- Fashion business growth supports earnings.

**Long-Term Outlook**
- Growth visibility remains strong beyond FY30.

**Key Takeaway**
- **HSBC upgraded Nykaa to Buy, citing its leadership in online beauty & personal care, increasing fashion market share, improving profitability, and strong long-term growth prospects extending beyond FY30.**

**Bajaj Auto | Brokerage Update (MOSL)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Buy (Upgraded).
- Target Price: ₹12,096.

**Q1 Assessment**
- Earnings beat estimates.
- Strong margins drove outperformance.
- Outlook remains positive across segments.

**Management Commentary**
- Targets ~2.50 lakh monthly exports from Q2.
- Export volume ramp-up expected.

**Cash Flow**
- FCF reached ₹2,300 Cr in Q1.
- FCF doubled YoY.
- Cash conversion at ~80% of PAT.

**Key Takeaway**
- **MOSL upgraded Bajaj Auto to Buy, citing strong margin-led earnings, robust free cash flow generation, improving export outlook, and positive growth prospects across business segments.**

**Indian Hotels | Brokerage Update (Morgan Stanley)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Equal-weight.
- Target Price: ₹783.

**Business Outlook**
- Q1 growth momentum expected in Q2.
- FY27 outlook remains firm.
- Standalone RevPAR met expectations.

**Guidance**
- Maintains double-digit FY27 revenue growth guidance.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Morgan Stanley remains constructive on Indian Hotels, expecting growth momentum to continue into Q2 with a firm FY27 outlook, supported by stable RevPAR performance and management's confidence in delivering double-digit revenue growth.**

**TVS Motor | Brokerage Update (Macquarie)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Outperform.
- Target Price: ₹4,325.

**Q1 Assessment**
- PAT beat estimates.
- Lower credit costs supported earnings.
- Margins remained resilient.
- Asset quality improved.

**Key Monitor**
- Sustainability of current performance.
- Margin and asset quality trends.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Macquarie remains positive on TVS Motor, citing better-than-expected Q1 earnings, resilient margins, and stronger asset quality, while highlighting the sustainability of these improvements as the key factor to watch going forward.**

**Bandhan Bank | Brokerage Update (Macquarie)**

**Rating & Target**
- Rating: Underperform.
- Target Price: ₹130.

**Q1 Assessment**
- PAT missed estimates.
- Margin outlook lowered.
- Credit costs remain elevated.

**Outlook**
- Recovery outlook remains uncertain.
- Earnings visibility remains weak.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Macquarie remains cautious on Bandhan Bank, citing weaker-than-expected earnings, pressure on margins, elevated credit costs, and an uncertain recovery trajectory, leading it to maintain an Underperform rating with a ₹130 target price.**

**M&M Financial Services | Morgan Stanley View**

**Brokerage View**
- **Equal-weight** maintained.
- Target price raised to **₹370**.
- Earlier target price **₹335**.
- Good Q1 performance.

**Q1 Highlights**
- PAT beat estimates by **7%**.
- Lower operating costs aided earnings.
- Lower credit costs supported profitability.
- Slightly higher NIM assumptions.

**Estimate Revisions**
- FY27–FY29 EPS raised by **8%**.
- Reflects improved cost outlook.
- Credit cost assumptions lowered.
- Margins expected to remain healthy.

**Valuation View**
- Higher target price assigned.
- Macro risks remain a consideration.
- Historical volatility acknowledged.
- Scope for valuation re-rating.

**Impact**

- Earnings outlook improved.
- Better profitability expectations.
- Valuation upside remains.

**Key Takeaway**

text:
India paid an additional ₹2.02 trillion for crude oil imports in the AMJ 2026 quarter.

This was higher by 77%, despite volume dropping by 4.5%.

Axis has come up with a Buy report on 
Steel Strip Wheels (SSWL) with a target price of 305.

However, 2 things to note - 
1> Their estimates are petty conservative, management guidance is actually higher with a positive bias. So, EPS could very well be 10-15% higher. 

2> They have valued the company at 14 PE.
I am not sure why. If the company cracks the export market and is able to grow at 20%+ with margin upside, I am not sure if it will trade at such low valuations.

SO, the bigger opportunity here is the valuation rerating.
If market decides that it deserves a 25-30 PE which is still lower than most auto ancillary stocks, then this can be a huge winner. Lets see how this goes.

text:
Goldman Sachs initiates coverage on Jindal Stainless 
BUY rating and TP at ₹1,000 

Key reasons behind the bullish stance:
✅ Best placed to benefit from rising domestic stainless steel consumption
✅ Higher share of value-added products to support profitability
✅ Strong balance sheet provides headroom to nearly double capacity from the current 4.2 MTPA over the medium term
✅ Attractive valuations relative to expected RoE

A side note: Amit Dixit the author of the report has been constructive on Jindal Stainless since much lower price levels during his time at previous brokerage houses.

**Paytm | CLSA View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- EBITDA reported at **₹200 crore**.
- EBITDA marginally beat estimates.
- Payments GMV growth accelerated to **31% YoY**.
- Financial services revenue growth strengthened.

**Growth Drivers**
- Merchant payments gaining momentum.
- Financial services scaling well.
- Stable contribution margin at **55%**.
- Lower fixed costs boosted profitability.

**Key Monitorables**
- Payment take rate declined sequentially.
- Lower doubtful debt provisions aided EBITDA.
- Wallet license application submitted.
- Higher operating expenses expected ahead.

**Brokerage View**
- **CLSA:** **Outperform**.
- Target Price **₹1,050**.
- FY27–FY29 EBITDA estimates cut **2–3%**.
- Limited upside after recent stock rally.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA acknowledges Paytm's strong operational momentum in payments and financial services, but believes much of the optimism around a possible UPI MDR return is already reflected in the stock price. While profitability continues to improve, higher operating expenses could moderate earnings growth.**

**Paytm | Citi View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Revenue grew **28% YoY**.
- EBITDA reached **₹200 crore**.
- EBITDA beat estimates by **16%**.
- Contribution profit at **55%** of revenue.

**Growth Drivers**
- Higher merchant loan distribution.
- Lower cloud infrastructure costs.
- Merchant ecosystem expanding.
- Operating leverage improving.

**Key Monitorables**
- Payment margins impacted by device rentals.
- Potential UPI MDR implementation.
- MDR could improve payment margins.
- Earnings estimates revised upward.

**Brokerage View**
- **Citi:** **Buy**.
- Target Price raised to **₹1,560**.
- Estimates increased after Q1.
- FY28 EBITDA could rise **8–10%** if UPI MDR is introduced.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi remains bullish on Paytm, supported by strong operating performance, improving cost efficiency and robust merchant lending growth. A potential introduction of UPI MDR on large merchants could provide an additional boost to payment margins and future earnings.**

**Paytm | Goldman Sachs View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Strong quarterly performance.
- Revenue growth accelerated.
- EBITDA beat estimates by **20%**.
- Bonus issue plans deferred.

**Growth Drivers**
- Offline payment market share gains.
- Online merchant additions accelerated.
- Merchant loan distribution remained strong.
- Consumer postpaid business expanding.

**Business Outlook**
- Payment ecosystem gaining momentum.
- Merchant acquisition continues.
- Consumer lending supports growth.
- Profitability expected to improve sharply.

**Brokerage View**
- **Goldman Sachs:** **Buy**.
- Target Price **₹1,500**.
- FY27 revenue growth estimated at **27% YoY**.
- FY27 EBITDA expected to more than double.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Goldman Sachs remains positive on Paytm, driven by accelerating payments growth, sustained merchant lending traction and expanding consumer financial products. The brokerage expects strong revenue momentum and a sharp improvement in profitability through FY27.**

**UltraTech Cement | Jefferies View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Strong execution continued.
- EBITDA grew **14% YoY**.
- Double-digit volume growth.
- Better pricing supported earnings.

**Growth Drivers**
- Healthy volume momentum.
- Stable pricing outlook.
- Strong operational execution.
- Capacity expansion progressing.

**Management Outlook**
- Pricing expected stable through monsoon.
- Peak industry costs anticipated.
- Double-digit volume growth expected in FY27.
- EBITDA/tonne target **₹1,400** by **Q4FY28**.

**Brokerage View**
- **Jefferies:** **Buy**.
- Target Price **₹14,065**.
- Positive on earnings trajectory.
- Confident on margin expansion.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains bullish on UltraTech Cement, citing superior execution, sustained double-digit volume growth, stable pricing and continued margin improvement. The brokerage expects the company to achieve ₹1,400 EBITDA per tonne by Q4FY28, supported by strong operational discipline and industry-leading scale.**

**UltraTech Cement | JPMorgan View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- PAT reported at **₹2,600 crore**.
- EBITDA/tonne at **₹1,214**.
- Volumes exceeded expectations.
- Quarter delivered a slight beat.

**Growth Drivers**
- Higher domestic volumes.
- Scale advantage remains strong.
- ICEM profitability improving.
- Market leadership intact.

**Key Monitorables**
- QoQ costs increased.
- Price hikes offset by costs.
- Q2 may remain challenging.
- Monsoon could impact demand.

**Management Outlook**
- ICEM EBITDA/tonne target **₹1,000**.
- Scale to support resilience.
- Better positioned than peers.
- Long-term outlook remains positive.

**Brokerage View**
- **JPMorgan:** **Overweight**.
- Target Price **₹13,300**.
- Positive on execution.
- Scale remains competitive advantage.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan remains constructive on UltraTech Cement, citing stronger-than-expected volume growth, improving ICEM profitability and unmatched scale, which should help the company outperform peers despite near-term cost pressures and seasonal demand challenges.**

**UltraTech Cement | Citi View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Consolidated EBITDA grew **14% YoY**.
- India volumes increased **13% YoY**.
- Realizations improved **1% YoY**.
- India EBITDA/tonne at **₹1,208**.

**Growth Drivers**
- Strong domestic volume growth.
- Realizations rose **~4% QoQ**.
- Cost management remained effective.
- ICL profitability improved significantly.

**Management Outlook**
- EBITDA/tonne target **₹1,400** by **Q4FY28E**.
- Q2 costs to rise **₹130–140/tonne**.
- Industry expects stable pricing.
- India expansion progressing as planned.

**Capital Allocation**
- Growth investments remain priority.
- Dividend policy unchanged.
- Net Debt/EBITDA expected below **1x**.
- Next expansion phase to be announced.

**Brokerage View**
- **Citi:** **Buy**.
- Target Price **₹14,750**.
- Positive on growth and cost control.
- Attractive valuation versus historical peak.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Citi remains bullish on UltraTech Cement, driven by strong volume growth, disciplined cost management, improving profitability and a robust expansion pipeline. Healthy balance sheet strength and continued capacity additions support sustained long-term earnings growth.**

**UltraTech Cement | CLSA View**

**Q1FY27 Highlights**
- Strong quarter across metrics.
- Volume grew **13% YoY**.
- EBITDA per tonne **₹1,183**.
- Performance beat industry trends.

**Growth Drivers**
- Better-than-expected cost control.
- Robust execution continued.
- Capacity expansion on track.
- Margin accretion expected.

**Management Outlook**
- Q2 cost increase guided at **₹130–140/tonne**.
- Lower than normal seasonal inflation.
- Earnings outlook remains constructive.
- Strong free cash flow expected.

**Brokerage View**
- **CLSA:** **High Conviction Outperform**.
- Target Price **₹14,000**.
- Mid-teen earnings CAGR expected over **FY26–30**.
- Improving ROCE supports valuation.

**Key Takeaway**
- **CLSA remains highly positive on UltraTech Cement, citing strong execution, superior cost control, ongoing capacity expansion and improving margins, which are expected to drive mid-teen earnings growth, higher ROCE and robust free cash flow over the medium term.**

**Oberoi Realty | HSBC View**

**Investment Thesis**
- Premium residential developer.
- Strong launch pipeline ahead.
- Healthy demand environment.
- Quality portfolio remains key strength.

**Growth Drivers**

**Brokerage View**

**Key Takeaway**
- **HSBC remains bullish on Oberoi Realty, believing the strong Gurugram launch and an accelerating pipeline of premium residential projects will drive sustained pre-sales growth and reinforce the company's leadership in the luxury real estate segment.**

**SBI Funds Management | Emkay View**

**Brokerage View**
- **Emkay:** **Buy (Initiation)**.
- Target Price **₹750**.
- Positive on brand strength.
- Favourable structural industry outlook.

**Investment Thesis**
- Leading domestic asset manager.
- Strong brand and distribution network.
- Large under-penetrated market opportunity.
- Well-positioned for long-term growth.

**Growth Drivers**
- Healthy AUM growth expected.

- Equity and alternate assets gaining share.
- Higher-yield asset mix supports margins.

**Financial Outlook**
- Operating leverage to strengthen earnings.
- Scale benefits improving profitability.
- EBITDA CAGR of **~17%** expected over **FY26–29**.
- Long-term growth visibility remains strong.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Emkay has initiated coverage on SBI Funds Management with a Buy rating, highlighting its dominant brand, extensive distribution network and strong structural tailwinds from rising financialisation. A shift toward higher-yielding asset classes and operating leverage are expected to drive sustained earnings growth.**

ACCIDENT OF THE DAY : WAAREE, PREMIER ENERGIES 

*ALMM-II extension, -ve for Solar mfg, +ve for developers* 

 *What’s new?* 
- MNRE, on Saturday, announced limited extension to the implementation of ALMM List-II (Approved List of Models & Manufacturers for solar cells) for Net Metering and Open Access (OA) solar projects to Dec-26 (earlier: May-26). 
- The decision followed by industry concerns that domestic cell manufacturing capacity remains insufficient to meet near-term demand, potentially delaying projects and increasing costs.
- MNRE stated that the move is intended to facilitate a smoother transition towards domestic cell sourcing while protecting investments already made by developers and module manufacturers.
- Interestingly, the order specifically notes that the extension will also help module manufacturers monetize existing inventories and provide time for the domestic solar cell industry to scale up capacity before the full implementation of ALMM List-II.

 *Our view* 
 
1) Impact on Solar Module Manufacturers (Waaree Energies & Premier Energies)
Near-term impact: Negative
- This extension reduces the value creation opportunity for the manufacturers such as Waaree Energies and Premier Energies with stronger backward intergration
- As the industry manufacturing capacity expands and demand supply mismatch reduces over the next six months, the pricing power for early movers of cell capacity additon would erode for the DCR based modules
- On the non DCR module, there can be an increase in demand in Q2 and Q3, however, given the lower realization and margin compared to DCR modules, this would still be net negative on the FY27 financials of these companies.

2) *Impact on CleanMax* 
 *Near term impact: Positive* 
- For RE open access and rooftop developers like CleanMax, this could have a significant boost in the segment as there can be an increased demand from consumer to benefit from the capex cost savings
- For C&I developer, execution certainty is often more valuable than marginal equipment localization benefits, making this policy amendment an incremental positive for growth and project returns over the next six months.

 *3) Industry impact* 
- In our view, the strong solar capacity addition in FY26 was partly driven by capacity additions ramp up to benefit prior to the change in regulations.
- We earlier, therefore had anticipated the spurt of FY26 (44.6GW addition) to subside marginally in FY27 and estimated a 35GW addition for the year.
- However, the announced extension, we see upside risk to our estimate. BROKERAGE NOTE

**Goldman Sachs | Auto Ancillary Sector Initiation**

**Sector View**
- Product mix transformation underway.
- Diversifying beyond auto cycles.
- Precision machining gaining traction.
- Higher-value businesses emerging.

**Industry Outlook**
- Revenue growth: **7%****/12****%****/10****%** (FY27E/28E/29E).
- EBITDA growth: **7%****/26****%****/14****%** (FY27E/28E/29E).
- Structural growth remains strong.
- Margin profile expected to improve.

**Top Picks**
- **Sansera Engineering:** **Buy**, TP **₹4,130**.
- **Craftsman Automation:** **Buy**, TP **₹11,600**.
- **Samvardhana Motherson:** **Neutral**, TP **₹148**.
- **Bharat Forge:** **Neutral**, TP **₹2,120**.

**Investment Thesis**
- Market views sector as cyclical.
- Pricing power seen improving.
- Manufacturing capabilities expanding.
- Transformation supports re-rating.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Goldman Sachs believes leading auto ancillary companies are evolving into diversified precision manufacturing businesses, reducing cyclicality and creating a stronger long-term earnings growth opportunity.**

**Tata Technologies | Q1FY27 JPMorgan Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- Revenue beat estimates.
- Margins missed expectations.
- Strong quarterly execution.
- Deal ramp-ups supported growth.

**Business Performance**
- Revenue grew **4.3% QoQ (CC)**.
- Services grew **4.3% QoQ (CC)**.
- Auto segment grew **4.3% QoQ (CC)**.
- Growth driven by deal ramp-ups.

**Growth Outlook**
- FY27 double-digit organic CC growth reiterated.
- Deal pipeline remains strong.
- German market faces headwinds.
- Client restructuring impacting demand.

**Brokerage View**
- **JPMorgan:** Underweight, TP **₹540**.
- Stock up **28%** since May.
- Valuation rich at **38x/33x FY27/28E P/E**.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan remains cautious on Tata Technologies despite strong revenue growth and management's confident outlook, citing German market headwinds and expensive valuations after the recent stock rally.**

**Oberoi Realty | JPMorgan Update**

**Business Performance**
- Commercial business resilient.
- Residential outlook strong.
- Pre-sales missed estimates.
- Down **21%** vs estimates.

**Financial Highlights**
- Negative free cash flow.
- Working capital driven.
- Funding costs increased QoQ.

**Growth Outlook**
- FY27/28 pre-sales may rise.
- Residential to drive growth.
- Sky City mall occupancy improved.
- Higher annuity NOI expected.

**Brokerage View**
- **JPMorgan:** Overweight, TP **₹2,050**.
- Positive long-term outlook.
- Commercial remains resilient.

**Key Takeaway**
- **JPMorgan remains positive on Oberoi Realty, expecting stronger residential sales next quarter and higher recurring income from improved Sky City mall occupancy despite weaker Q1 pre-sales and temporary cash flow pressure.**

**CEAT | Q1FY27 Nomura Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA missed estimates.
- Margins remained under pressure.
- Near-term profitability subdued.

**Growth Outlook**
- Price hikes to aid margins.
- Recovery expected in 2HFY27.
- Premium segment focus.
- Export growth continues.

**Valuation**
- Trading at **7.1x FY28 EV/EBITDA**.
- Valuation seen attractive.
- EBITDA CAGR **16%** (FY25-29).

**Brokerage View**
- **Nomura:** Buy, TP **₹4,266**.
- Positive long-term outlook.
- Margin recovery expected.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Nomura remains bullish on CEAT, expecting price hikes, premiumisation and export growth to drive margin recovery and a 16% EBITDA CAGR despite a weak Q1FY27.**

**CEAT | Q1FY27 Kotak Institutional Equities Update**

**Financial Highlights**
- EBITDA **₹365 crore**.
- Down **6% YoY**.
- Down **38% QoQ**.
- Missed estimates by **13%**.

**Business Performance**
- India business remained weak.
- Camso performance disappointed.
- Domestic execution stayed healthy.
- Acquisition risks persist.

**Outlook**
- Weak profitability in Q2.
- Raw material headwinds continue.
- Currency pressures remain.
- Recovery expected in 2HFY27.

**Brokerage View**
- **Kotak Institutional Equities:** Reduce, TP **₹3,350**.
- Cautious near-term outlook.
- Camso remains key risk.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Kotak remains cautious on CEAT as weaker-than-expected profitability, raw material inflation and Camso integration challenges are likely to weigh on earnings before an expected recovery in 2HFY27.**

**Adani Green Energy | Macquarie Update**

**Growth Outlook**
- BESS added to estimates.
- **10 GWh** annual additions.
- EBITDA upside expected.
- Execution remains strong.

**Capacity Expansion**
- Commissioned **5 GW+** in FY26.
- Operational capacity **20 GW+**.
- **5.5 GW/year** additions till FY30.
- Khavda targets **30 GW**.

**Earnings Outlook**
- Base-case EBITDA CAGR **30%**.
- Bull-case CAGR **40%**.
- FY28/29 EBITDA raised **6%****/10****%**.
- EPS impacted by higher interest.

**Brokerage View**
- **Macquarie:** Outperform, TP **₹1,800** *(raised)*.
- Positive on BESS.
- Long-term growth intact.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Macquarie remains highly bullish on Adani Green, citing rapid renewable capacity additions, BESS integration and the Khavda mega project as key drivers of 30–40% EBITDA growth over the next five years.**

**Kotak Mahindra Bank | Q1FY27 Jefferies Update**

**Financial Highlights**

- Up **26% YoY**.
- Beat estimates.
- Better NII and fees.

**Business Performance**
- Loans grew **15%**.
- NII up **9%**.
- Deposits grew **12%**.

**Asset Quality**
- Credit quality improved.
- MFI trends strengthened.

**Brokerage View**
- **Jefferies:** Buy, TP **₹460**.

- CEO succession key monitorable.
- Valuation remains reasonable.

**Key Takeaway**
- **Jefferies remains positive on Kotak Mahindra Bank, supported by improving credit quality, healthy earnings growth and better fee income, while CEO succession remains the key near-term monitorable.**
